ddls:
CREATE TABLE "io_units" (
  "io_unit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each I/O unit */,
  "unit_name" TEXT /* Name of the I/O unit */,
  "unit_type" TEXT /* Type of the I/O unit (e.g., Digital, Analog, Network) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the I/O unit */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the I/O unit in the facility */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the I/O unit was installed */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date the I/O unit was last updated */,
  "serial_number" TEXT /* Unique serial number for each I/O unit */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Name of the manufacturer */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model number of the I/O unit */,
  "firmware_version" TEXT /* Current firmware version of the I/O unit */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current operational status (e.g., Active, Inactive, Maintenance) */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance */,
  "next_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the next scheduled maintenance */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who created the I/O unit entry */,
  "last_updated_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who last updated the I/O unit entry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("io_unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "io_signals" (
  "signal_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each I/O signal */,
  "io_unit_id" INTEGER /* ID of the I/O unit the signal is mapped to */,
  "signal_name" TEXT /* Name of the I/O signal */,
  "signal_type" INTEGER /* Type of the I/O signal (e.g., Boolean, Integer, Float) */,
  "bit_count" INTEGER /* Number of bits the signal occupies */,
  "bit_offset" INTEGER /* Offset of the first bit of the signal in the I/O memory map */,
  "bit_order" INTEGER /* Order of the bits in the signal (MSBFirst or LSBFirst) */,
  "octet_offset" INTEGER /* Octet number in the I/O memory map where the signal starts */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the I/O signal */,
  "use_limitations" TEXT /* Use limitations for the I/O signal */,
  "signal_status" TEXT /* Current status of the signal (e.g., Active, Inactive, Fault) */,
  "min_value" REAL /* Minimum value the signal can take */,
  "max_value" REAL /* Maximum value the signal can take */,
  "unit_of_measurement" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the signal */,
  "last_read_value" REAL /* Last known value of the signal */,
  "last_read_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the last read */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who created the signal entry */,
  "last_updated_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who last updated the signal entry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("signal_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_io_signals_signal_type FOREIGN KEY ("signal_type") REFERENCES signal_types ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_io_signals_io_unit_id FOREIGN KEY ("io_unit_id") REFERENCES io_units ("io_unit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_io_signals_bit_order FOREIGN KEY ("bit_order") REFERENCES signal_bit_orders ("bit_order_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "signal_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each signal type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the signal type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the signal type */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Data type of the signal (e.g., Boolean, Integer, Float) */,
  "min_value" REAL /* Minimum value for the signal type */,
  "max_value" REAL /* Maximum value for the signal type */,
  "unit_of_measurement" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the signal type */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who created the signal type entry */,
  "last_updated_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who last updated the signal type entry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "signal_bit_orders" (
  "bit_order_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each bit order type */,
  "bit_order_name" TEXT /* Name of the bit order type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the bit order type */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who created the bit order type entry */,
  "last_updated_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who last updated the bit order type entry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("bit_order_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "io_unit_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "io_unit_id" INTEGER /* ID of the I/O unit the log entry is related to */,
  "log_date" TEXT /* Date of the log entry */,
  "log_type" TEXT /* Type of the log entry (e.g., Error, Warning, Info) */,
  "log_message" TEXT /* Message of the log entry */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the log entry (e.g., Critical, High, Medium, Low) */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who created the log entry */,
  "last_updated_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who last updated the log entry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_io_unit_logs_io_unit_id FOREIGN KEY ("io_unit_id") REFERENCES io_units ("io_unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., Engineer, Operator, Admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "password" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "last_login_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the last login */,
  "last_password_change_date" TEXT /* Date of the last password change */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the user account is active */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who created the user entry */,
  "last_updated_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who last updated the user entry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "io_unit_id" INTEGER /* ID of the I/O unit being accessed */,
  "signal_id" INTEGER /* ID of the I/O signal being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the I/O unit or signal */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the I/O unit or signal was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, modify, delete) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used for access */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who created the access log entry */,
  "last_updated_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the person who last updated the access log entry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_signal_id FOREIGN KEY ("signal_id") REFERENCES io_signals ("signal_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_io_unit_id FOREIGN KEY ("io_unit_id") REFERENCES io_units ("io_unit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
